resources.string: Generate a single realistic office-life walking pattern, write it to Health Connect, and preview both historical totals and the next 7 projected days.
Grant Health Connect step read/write permission

Weekdays follow an office routine almost all the time: early wake-up, commute walking, very little movement during work hours, lunch out only on some days, the walk home, and occasional evening runs. Weekends usually start later, but some weekends still begin early, with longer outings and lighter social walking mixed in.
Minimum daily steps from Schrittji only
Maximum daily steps from Schrittji only
Enable battery-aware background top-ups (WorkManager, about every 15 minutes)
Generated step data written to Health Connect
Projected full-day totals for the next 7 calendar days using Schrittji\'s fixed real-life schedule generator.
Evening run window in the generated schedule.
Cycling-style outing or commute window in the generated schedule.
No dedicated run or outing window on this day; walking is mixed across the day.
Health Connect stores total steps; activity icons reflect the day pattern Schrittji uses when comparing to projection.
Projection uses Schrittji minute records that follow the same run and outing windows.
Loading step data from Health Connect…
No step records were returned by Health Connect.
No step entries exist for this day.

Notes: the generator uses one repeatable office-life pattern with layered random variation, writes minute-sized step records into Health Connect, and uses WorkManager instead of an always-on service so background top-ups stay system-managed and energy efficient.
